title: Catherine

jokes.text: "who's the real first lady?" sexist bullshit. can't get drawn into it. comparing our clothing budgets and our stylists-- like that's all we care about?
jokes.setup: Selina complains about sexist media coverage focusing on superficial things
jokes.punchline: The irony that she's immediately about to obsess over those exact same things
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep hypocrisy - character condemns something while embodying it. Well-structured irony.

jokes.text: stupid fucking midget. clearly he hasn't heard you sing.
jokes.setup: Selina calls columnist Mark Rivera a 'stupid fucking midget' for calling her a diva
jokes.punchline: Gary's earnest 'clearly he hasn't heard you sing' - treating the diva accusation as factual
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary obliviousness - he agrees she's a diva while trying to defend her. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: or should i say canine-didates?
jokes.setup: Gary mentioning he found dog candidates
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on candidates/canine-didates
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but fits Gary's earnest, bad-joke character. Gets immediate rejection from others.

jokes.text: no, you shouldn't say that.
jokes.setup: Gary's terrible canine-didates pun
jokes.punchline: Selina's flat rejection of the wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clean straight-man response. Economy and timing work well.

jokes.text: i don't need a ball of yappy hair shitting its body weight every 30 minutes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of getting a terrier dog
jokes.punchline: Vivid, crude description of small dog behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina vulgarity with specific, visual imagery. Quotable line.

jokes.text: four legs good; three legs bad.
jokes.setup: Her comment about three-legged dogs
jokes.punchline: Reference to Animal Farm's 'four legs good, two legs bad'
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that adds intellectual layer to the crude setup.

jokes.text: simon? what an unbelievable name. i guess he just looked like a simon.
jokes.setup: Mike reveals his dog is named Simon
jokes.punchline: Selina's mock amazement at such a mundane dog name
jokes.explanation: Selina's sarcastic incredulity at Mike's boring normalcy.

jokes.text: to drink or applied directly to the coat?
jokes.setup: Mike says he keeps his dog's coat shiny with lots of water
jokes.punchline: Selina asking if water is consumed or topically applied
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - takes Mike's simple answer in unexpected direction.

jokes.text: ha! leave my fake dog alone.
jokes.setup: Everyone's questions about Mike's dog Simon
jokes.punchline: Mike accidentally reveals his dog is fake
jokes.explanation: Excellent slip that reveals a running character trait. Sets up major callback later.

jokes.text: four legs good; three legs bad.
jokes.setup: Discussion of rescue dogs, some with disabilities
jokes.punchline: Riff on 'Animal Farm' - 'four legs good, two legs bad'
jokes.explanation: Literary reference works but feels slightly forced for Selina's character.

jokes.text: whassup, as they say in the late '90s?
jokes.setup: Jonah arriving at the event
jokes.punchline: Dating himself by referencing 'wassup' as late 90s (it was early 2000s)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - trying to be cool but getting details wrong. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: although i'm sure he wouldn't miss it for the world if it weren't for the fact that he runs the world.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the President can't attend Selina's party
jokes.punchline: Overblown explanation that he 'runs the world'
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah grandiosity but verbose. Character-appropriate but not sharp.

jokes.text: she'd be a rough fighter, though. she's got big shoulders. those aren't pads.
jokes.setup: Discussion of alleged catfight with First Lady
jokes.punchline: Analyzing First Lady's physical fighting advantages
jokes.explanation: Good Selina cattiness - specific visual detail makes it work.

jokes.text: blink if you understand me, mike.
jokes.setup: Mike not responding appropriately to questions
jokes.punchline: Treating Mike like he's mentally impaired
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina condescension. Economy and character integration strong.

jokes.text: be careful your ears don't pop on the way down.
jokes.setup: Jonah saying he'll keep his ear to the ground
jokes.punchline: Selina warning about altitude change from his height
jokes.explanation: Clever height joke with good setup. Classic Selina wit with perfect timing.

jokes.text: i was gonna say eva peron. yeah, i prefer that comparison. less of a mustache.
jokes.setup: Comparison between Stalin and Eva Peron for large photos
jokes.punchline: Selina preferring Eva Peron because 'less of a mustache'
jokes.explanation: Good vanity joke - Selina chooses dictator based on appearance concerns.

jokes.text: this is not the hoover dam. yeah, it is the hoover dam... filled with shit.
jokes.setup: Amy saying announcing Chuck isn't like building Hoover Dam
jokes.punchline: Mike agreeing but adding 'filled with shit'
jokes.explanation: Mike trying to contribute but making it worse. Character-appropriate incompetence.

jokes.text: uh, does it get used in a clumsy and unpleasant analogy by you?
jokes.setup: Mike setting up analogy about what happens to 'shit'
jokes.punchline: Amy predicting Mike will make a bad analogy
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor. Amy calling out Mike's tendency for bad analogies before he makes one.

jokes.text: i'm like the last guy in a human centipede with this. and there it is.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining her political position
jokes.punchline: Grotesque human centipede analogy, followed by Amy's 'and there it is'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Amy's prediction. Disgusting but effective analogy.

jokes.text: he was rapey reeves. when was that guy ever respectful?
jokes.setup: Discussion of respecting Senator Reeves at his memorial
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to him being 'rapey'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of the show. Mike's casual delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: god, today feels like the perfect storm, doesn't it? yeah, hurricane selina.
jokes.setup: Selina comparing her day to a perfect storm
jokes.punchline: Gary connecting it to literal Hurricane Selina
jokes.explanation: Good connection between metaphor and literal plot point.

jokes.text: and just so you know, this is also what would happen if we were in a lifeboat.
jokes.setup: Kicking Dan and Jonah out of the room
jokes.punchline: Comparing office hierarchy to lifeboat survival priorities
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about office dynamics. Quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: selina is on next year's list of hurricanes. oh, shit! what if it hits and we get headlines saying 'selina causing large-scale devastation'?
jokes.setup: Discovery that Hurricane Selina is a real upcoming storm
jokes.punchline: Selina's paranoid projection about negative headlines
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina paranoia - making natural disaster about her image.

jokes.text: people won't equate you with a natural disaster, ma'am. really, amy? 'cause i've met some people, okay? real people, and i've got to tell you, a lot of them are fucking idiots.
jokes.setup: Amy reassuring Selina people won't make the hurricane connection
jokes.punchline: Selina's cynical assessment of voter intelligence
jokes.explanation: Peak Selina cynicism about voters. Perfectly captures her worldview and quotable.

jokes.text: did you know old grandpa fumblepants couldn't even swim? he would just hang out in the shallow end and stare. you know what his favorite stroke was? was it dick stroke? it was dick stroke.
jokes.setup: Discussing dead Senator Reeves' swimming habits
jokes.punchline: Sexual pun on swimming stroke name
jokes.explanation: Juvenile pun but fits the show's tone. Somewhat predictable setup.

jokes.text: f.y.i., ma'am, the president is not calling. f.y.i., gary, no shit.
jokes.setup: Gary stating obvious that fake phone call is fake
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic 'no shit' response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Gary's obliviousness vs. Selina's exasperation.

jokes.text: you are like a... earlobe-- you're just there, just wobbling.
jokes.setup: Selina struggling to find insult for Mike
jokes.punchline: Settling on comparing him to a useless wobbling earlobe
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with specific visual imagery. Selina's struggle to find the right insult adds to it.

jokes.text: oh, holy shit. these mushrooms are amazing. what is it, mike? must be ricotta or something in there.
jokes.setup: Mike supposed to discuss serious political matter with Senator
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by appetizers instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike incompetence - completely missing the point of the conversation.

jokes.text: so i imagine that i would mix ape shit with bat shit, raise it to a whole new level of fury, then i'd probably rip your face off and use your eye sockets as a sex toy.
jokes.setup: Mike asking hypothetically about Chuck Furnham appointment
jokes.punchline: Doyle's extremely violent and sexual response
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation with memorable imagery. Peak Veep vulgarity with specific, visceral details.

jokes.text: it's just a hypothetical like yours.
jokes.setup: Doyle's violent threat about ripping face off
jokes.punchline: Mike treating extreme violence as equivalent to his mild hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike obliviousness to tone. Great understatement after extreme threat.

jokes.text: you dumped my daughter by text and didn't even apologize. oh, no, i did. i signed off with colon open brackets.
jokes.setup: Accusation Dan didn't apologize for dumping daughter
jokes.punchline: Dan thinking emoticon counts as apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan callousness disguised as technicality. Character-perfect response.

jokes.text: chuck is a gesture all right. do you know what these oil wise guys call him? they call him 'who the fuck is chuck furnham?'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Chuck being a 'gesture' to oil industry
jokes.punchline: Oil industry's dismissive non-recognition
jokes.explanation: Good political insight joke - captures Washington insider knowledge.

jokes.text: your husband was a massive player. politically speaking, of course.
jokes.setup: Complimenting dead Senator Reeves to his widow
jokes.punchline: Clarifying 'player' was political, implying sexual connotation
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre with awkward save. Selina's social awkwardness.

jokes.text: i guess all those tears must be dehydrating.
jokes.setup: Widow asking for water
jokes.punchline: Jonah's inappropriate logical explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah inappropriate logic. Character-perfect insensitivity.

jokes.text: cock-blocking widow.
jokes.setup: Widow interrupting Jonah's attempted flirtation
jokes.punchline: Crude characterization of grieving widow
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah crudeness. Economy of expression but cheap humor.

jokes.text: is there even a name for that? how about catch-44?
jokes.setup: Two simultaneous Catch-22 situations
jokes.punchline: Gary's mathematical solution: Catch-44
jokes.explanation: Good Gary logic - overly literal solution. Clean mathematical humor.

jokes.text: if you say chuck furnham, i will go into anaphylactic fucking shock.
jokes.setup: Amy about to mention Chuck Furnham's name
jokes.punchline: Extreme medical reaction threat to hearing the name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with medical metaphor. Sets up immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: i'm saying chuck furnham. fucking hell!
jokes.setup: Sidney's threat about anaphylactic shock from hearing name
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately says the name, Sidney's explosive reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate payoff to setup. Amy's defiance and Sidney's reaction.

jokes.text: the only thing that chuck is plugged into is his fucking piss bag.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Chuck being 'plugged in' to oil industry
jokes.punchline: Literal medical interpretation of 'plugged in'
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective misdirection from political to medical terminology.

jokes.text: this in no way will be demeaning. just pretend you're picking him up, you know, and he's blind.
jokes.setup: Asking Mike to babysit Chuck in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Claiming non-demeaning while describing exactly demeaning scenario
jokes.explanation: Good Selina self-awareness paired with complete disregard for Mike's dignity.

jokes.text: now is it true that the first lady calls you creepy veepy?
jokes.setup: Widow asking about First Lady rumors
jokes.punchline: Rhyming nickname 'creepy veepy'
jokes.explanation: Childish rhyming nickname but memorable and character-damaging.

jokes.text: as good as announced is not announced. you are as good as dead, but you're not actually dead... not yet.
jokes.setup: Chuck thinking his appointment is 'as good as announced'
jokes.punchline: Amy's death threat analogy with ominous ending
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with dark humor. Amy's precision in explanation with threat.

jokes.text: tell her that i'm cancelling the lunch that was supposed to prove there's nothing more important than catherine because something more important than catherine has come up.
jokes.setup: Selina needing to cancel lunch with daughter
jokes.punchline: Explicitly stating the contradiction in her message
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina self-awareness with complete disregard. Excellent structure and character integration.

jokes.text: you guys ditched me at a pervert's memorial that's some cold shit.
jokes.setup: Dan returning after being abandoned
jokes.punchline: Characterizing memorial as 'pervert's memorial'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier 'rapey Reeves' comment. Dan's casual crudeness.

jokes.text: a rubik's cube is not impossible to solve. i saw a chinese kid do it in like 10 seconds. 10 seconds, mike.
jokes.setup: Mike calling their situation impossible like a Rubik's cube
jokes.punchline: Selina's literal correction with racist stereotype
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina literal-mindedness but relies on stereotype. Character-appropriate but cheap.

jokes.text: yeah, it looks astonished. like it's attached to jumper cables or something.
jokes.setup: Looking at Catherine's chosen dog photo
jokes.punchline: Description of dog's shocked expression
jokes.explanation: Good visual description humor. Specific and vivid imagery.

jokes.text: yeah, it looks astonished. like it's attached to jumper cables or something
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of dog Catherine chose
jokes.punchline: Specific description of dog's shocked expression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual description that's very specific

jokes.text: what is that skirt? is it a rug?
jokes.setup: Selina critiquing Catherine's outfit
jokes.punchline: Comparing daughter's clothing to home furnishing
jokes.explanation: Harsh but character-appropriate mother criticism. Good cringe factor.

jokes.text: you got to be fucking kidding me. i hate these animals. this thing is... unbelievably cute.
jokes.setup: Selina seeing the chosen dog
jokes.punchline: Contradiction between hatred and admission of cuteness
jokes.explanation: Good Selina contradiction - her principles vs. visceral reaction.

jokes.text: 20 years ago, you had no power, but you had balls. now look at you. yeah, now look at me. i have a dick and balls, okay?
jokes.setup: Doyle saying Selina used to have balls
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude anatomical escalation
jokes.explanation: Selina's defensive vulgarity. Character-appropriate but somewhat crude humor.

jokes.text: no, please. don't stop because of me. this is really interesting
jokes.setup: Adults stopping argument because Catherine is there
jokes.punchline: Catherine encouraging them to continue
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of adult behavior around kids

jokes.text: you could have said, 'don't blow it.' no, i'm not gonna say that.
jokes.setup: Gary mentioning trumpet player has recital
jokes.punchline: Gary suggesting obvious pun, Selina refusing
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about obvious puns. Gary's bad joke instincts vs. Selina's standards.

jokes.text: he's like a human teleprompter for small talk. he calls this gary-oke.
jokes.setup: Gary feeding Selina conversation prompts
jokes.punchline: Gary's portmanteau of his name and karaoke
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and perfect character insight. Good Gary/Selina dynamic observation.

jokes.text: as opposed to what, the west wing of graceland?
jokes.setup: Jonah specifying he works at 'west wing of the white house'
jokes.punchline: Catherine pointing out redundancy with Graceland comparison
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback showing Catherine's intelligence. Good burn on Jonah's pretentiousness.

jokes.text: it's kind of an open secret in washington that mike has an imaginary dog. we call it his bullshit-tzu.
jokes.setup: Explaining Mike's fake dog situation
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'bullshit' and 'shih tzu' breed
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun and callback. Memorable portmanteau that becomes recurring joke.

jokes.text: that's kind of genius. thanks, todd
jokes.setup: Catherine commenting on Mike's fake dog scheme
jokes.punchline: Person thanking her despite wrong name
jokes.explanation: Minor moment of social awkwardness

jokes.text: last two on the deck of the titanic, huh? yeah, i think i might jump.
jokes.setup: Mike comparing them to last people at party
jokes.punchline: Catherine preferring suicide to talking to Mike
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good setup. Catherine's deadpan response to Mike's small talk.

jokes.text: dilka. what? dilka? dilka? i think her dad's iranian. iranian?
jokes.setup: Catherine mentioning her roommate's name
jokes.punchline: Selina's escalating concern about the foreign-sounding name
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's xenophobic tendencies and political paranoia about her daughter's associations.

jokes.text: sweet hairy fuck.
jokes.setup: Learning that Sidney Purcell is now on the task force
jokes.punchline: Doyle's uniquely vulgar exclamation
jokes.explanation: Memorable and creative profanity that stands out even in Veep's profanity-heavy dialogue.

jokes.text: you are not fucking thor, mom.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to control hurricane naming
jokes.punchline: Catherine's mythological reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine calling out absurdity. Excellent economy and character moment.

jokes.text: mom, stop talking to me like a fucking politician.
jokes.setup: Selina giving political non-answers to her daughter
jokes.punchline: Catherine explicitly calling out the behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on Selina's inability to be genuine even with family.

jokes.text: mom, stop talking to me like a fucking politician.
jokes.setup: Selina using political speak with her daughter
jokes.punchline: Catherine calling her out for treating family like constituents
jokes.explanation: Cuts to the heart of Selina's problem - she can't turn off the political persona even with family.

jokes.text: when you say it out loud, you know, it does sound-- kind of idiotic.
jokes.setup: Selina pointing out Doyle's contradictory requests
jokes.punchline: Doyle's reluctant self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good moment of political self-awareness. Character admits absurdity.

jokes.text: catch-44, he caught it. pow!
jokes.setup: Resolving the double Catch-22 situation
jokes.punchline: Gary celebrating his earlier mathematical joke
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke showing Gary's pride in his contribution, though the original joke was weak.

jokes.text: ma'am, you need to kill the dog. not literally, but-- i mean, yeah, if it comes to it, then literally.
jokes.setup: Jonah suggesting canceling dog adoption
jokes.punchline: Escalating from metaphorical to literal dog murder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke. Jonah's casual progression to violence is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: you guys, are we seriously gonna let the guy with the police sketch face of a rapist tell us what to do?
jokes.setup: Team considering Jonah's dog advice
jokes.punchline: Catherine's brutal description of Jonah's appearance
jokes.explanation: Savage and specific insult. Catherine showing her mother's mean streak with better accuracy.

jokes.text: oh, this rapist face gets eights. consensually, i might throw in.
jokes.setup: Catherine insulting Jonah's appearance
jokes.punchline: Jonah's inappropriate sexual boast as defense
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah inappropriate response. Character-perfect but crude defense mechanism.

jokes.text: you are getting this little rat-faced, repulsive, yappy, crepit-looking terrier.
jokes.setup: Selina forcing Mike to adopt the dog
jokes.punchline: String of increasingly harsh descriptors for the cute dog
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and contradiction to earlier praise. Selina's spite overriding honesty.

jokes.text: it's called a shitbull terrier. it's a bullshit-tzu.
jokes.setup: Discussing Mike's fake dog
jokes.punchline: Competing puns for Mike's imaginary dog breed
jokes.explanation: Good callback with competing puns. Both work well as portmanteaus.

jokes.text: there's not enough alcohol in the world, jonah.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking Sue about romantic availability
jokes.punchline: Sue's absolute rejection with specific impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue deadpan rejection. Economy and specificity make it memorable.

jokes.text: you got a convenient opening i can slide into? open up a slot for jonah? there's not enough alcohol in the world, jonah.
jokes.setup: Jonah's sexually suggestive pickup attempt
jokes.punchline: Sue's brutal shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect rejection line that's both final and creative. Shows Sue's quick wit against Jonah's creepiness.

jokes.text: let's go mingle the shit out of 'em.
jokes.setup: Selina preparing to work the party
jokes.punchline: Turning mingling into aggressive action
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina aggressive approach to social obligations. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: let's go mingle the shit out of 'em.
jokes.setup: Deciding to go work the party
jokes.punchline: Selina's aggressive approach to socializing
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina treating socializing like a competitive sport rather than genuine interaction.

jokes.text: that must have hurt... unless you had a c-section.
jokes.setup: Meeting someone who just had triplets
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate medical speculation about childbirth pain
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina inappropriate comment at a social gathering, showing her lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: su casa es mi casa. mi casa. mi casa es su casa.
jokes.setup: Gary telling her to use Spanish with Carlos
jokes.punchline: Selina getting the common phrase backwards
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's superficial multiculturalism and inability to get basic phrases right.

jokes.text: oh, christ has risen.
jokes.setup: Gary prompting about reverend who's also a baker
jokes.punchline: Religious pun about bread rising
jokes.explanation: Predictable religious/baking pun but fits Gary's earnest bad-joke character.

jokes.text: tell your brother i love his music. no, he's not.
jokes.setup: Gary's incorrect information about a guest's brother
jokes.punchline: Gary immediately contradicting his own briefing
jokes.explanation: Shows Gary's poor briefing skills and tendency to give bad information then immediately correct it.

jokes.text: oh, christ has risen.
jokes.setup: Meeting a baker from the Church of the Living Christ
jokes.punchline: Bread rising pun with religious reference
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun combining the religious context with baking, but executed efficiently.

tmdb_overview: Selina celebrates 20 years in Washington, D.C., but tries to keep a low profile after a newspaper article paints her as a diva and rumors float about her feud with the first lady. Meanwhile, Selina’s college-age daughter arrives for a visit, and is surprised that her mother is looking for a dog but appalled by her reaction to “Selina” being on the list of names for hurricanes.
insight: Political incompetence peaks here: 78 jokes in 39 minutes, mostly cringe-fueled character disasters.
